description_long: This is a twin-boom, fixed-wing airplane with a distinctive high-wing configuration and a forward-mounted cockpit. The fuselage is streamlined and painted primarily in olive green with red accents on the nose, engine cowlings, and tail tips. The aircraft features two propeller engines mounted on the wings, each with visible twin-blade propellers. The tail section is an H-tail design, with two vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear is fixed and appears to be a tricycle configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel. The cockpit is enclosed with blue-tinted windows and is positioned at the very front of the fuselage.
description: A twin-boom, high-wing airplane with two propeller engines, an H-tail, and a forward, enclosed cockpit in olive green and red.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red nose, blue cockpit windows, twin propellers, and high-mounted wings with visible landing gear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the streamlined fuselage, twin booms, and the fixed tricycle landing gear beneath the wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and the connecting horizontal stabilizer.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cockpit placement, engine nacelles, and the symmetry of the twin-boom structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles, mounted above the fuselage and twin booms.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels under the wings and a single nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with blue-tinted windows, likely single or dual-seat.
